title: Don't handle AstSelBit if the fromp() is unresolved
body:
It fixes the handling of select operations on unresolved objects.
Currently on master, if we have a select operation on unresolved object in a dot reference, e. g `q[i].x;` from the test I added, it is converted to `AstCellArrayRef`.

The problem was caused by setting m_ds.m_dotPos to DP_SCOPE in AstDot* handling and then keeping this value if the reference is unresolvable. I think that if the symbol of AstParseRef* was a scope, it would be found. So if we didn't find a definition of a symbol and we are under a class with parameterized `extends`, the symbol means an object or a function, so I assign the next dot position, which is a member.
